title: Investigate an OOM kill
description: Correlate the kernel OOM log, RSS growth, and allocation paths to find the unbounded consumer.
body: Steps: 1. Confirm an OOM kill from the kernel log (dmesg/journal: 'Out of memory: Killed process') and note which process was killed. 2. Reconstruct memory growth (RSS over time, heap profiler) to see whether it is a leak, unbounded buffer, or large single allocation. 3. Identify the allocation site and whether input size drives it. 4. Bound it: stream the input, cap the cache, or add backpressure. 5. Re-run the load to confirm RSS plateaus. Rollback point: read-only diagnosis; the bounding change is reversible. Validation: RSS stays flat under the load that previously triggered the kill and no further OOM events occur.
